title: Apollo 11
text: Apollo 11 was the American spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon. Commander Neil Armstrong and Lunar Module Pilot Buzz Aldrin landed the Apollo Lunar Module Eagle on July 20, 1969, at 20:17 UTC, and Armstrong became the first person to step onto the Moon's surface six hours and 39 minutes later, on July 21 at 02:56 UTC. Aldrin joined him 19 minutes later, and they spent about two and a quarter hours together exploring the site they had named Tranquility Base upon landing. Armstrong and Aldrin collected 47.5 pounds (21.5 kg) of lunar material to bring back to Earth. Command Module Pilot Michael Collins flew the Command Module Columbia alone in lunar orbit while they were on the Moon's surface. Armstrong, Aldrin and Collins are the crew of Apollo 11. The mission was launched by NASA from Kennedy Space Center in Florida on July 16, 1969, UTC.

title: NASA
text: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) is an independent agency of the US federal government responsible for the civil space program, aeronautics research, and space research. It was established in 1958, succeeding the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics (NACA). NASA has since led most American space exploration efforts, including Project Mercury, Project Gemini, the Apollo Moon landing missions, the Skylab space station, and later the Space Shuttle program. Currently, NASA supports the International Space Station and is overseeing the development of the Orion spacecraft, the Space Launch System, and the Commercial Crew vehicles. The agency also manages the Launch Services Program which provides launch oversight of NASA fleet launches. NASA's science is focused on better understanding Earth through the Earth Observing System, advancing heliophysics through the efforts of the Science Mission Directorate, exploring bodies throughout the Solar System with advanced robotic spacecraft, and researching astrophysics topics, such as the Big Bang, through the Great Observatories and associated programs.

title: SpaceX
text: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., commonly known as SpaceX, is an American spacecraft manufacturer, launch service provider, and satellite communications company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. The company was founded in 2002 by Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs to enable the colonization of Mars. SpaceX manufactures the Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy launch vehicles, several rocket engines, the Cargo Dragon and Crew Dragon spacecraft, and Starlink communications satellites. SpaceX is credited with the first privately funded liquid-propellant rocket to reach orbit (Falcon 1), the first privately funded company to successfully launch, orbit, and recover a spacecraft (Dragon), the first private company to send astronauts to the International Space Station, and the first to achieve a vertical propulsive landing for an orbital rocket booster. NASA has awarded SpaceX contracts to provide crew transportation to the ISS as part of the Commercial Crew Development program.
